mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 4.0% show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p > 0.10 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns in periods with HOUST > 1400k and RSAFS > 370k show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p > 0.10 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 112.0 signals robust manufacturing activity that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with INDPRO > 112.0 show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p > 0.10 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 2.0% signals deflationary risks that prompt central bank intervention, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL < 2.0% show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p > 0.10 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2.5% annually signals economic weakness that triggers risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with GDPC1 < 2.5% show positive or flat performance (mean > 0.1%) with p > 0.10 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strong labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 137k and UNRATE < 4.5% show negative or flat performance (mean < 0.1%) with p > 0.10 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 5.0% during an inflationary environment (CPIAUCSL > 3.0%) signals aggressive monetary tightening that depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and CPIAUCSL > 3.0% show positive or flat performance (mean > 0.1%) with p > 0.10 in t-test against baseline.
